title: bridelia ovata var. genuina
intro: Bridelia ovata var. genuina, also known as the False Calumba, is a plant species native to tropical Asia, characterized by its small, yellowish flowers and red, oval-shaped fruits. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Bridelia ovata var. genuina include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, reduce cholesterol and triglycerides, and exhibit anti-cancer properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in herbal preparations such as capsules and tinctures, often in combination with other medicinal plants.
